headline: Orang Asli Land Titles Cover Only 0.14% of Claimed Customary Territory
context: JAKOA data shows registered Orang Asli land titles total 18,940 hectares against estimated customary territory claims of 13.5 million hectares — a coverage rate of 0.14%.

cards.big: Orang Asli claim customary rights to 13.5 million hectares.
cards.sub: Registered titles cover 18,940 hectares — 0.14%. At the current titling rate, full coverage would take 2,800 years.

cards.big: The Federal Court has recognised Orang Asli customary land rights in 5 landmark cases.
cards.sub: Despite these rulings, the administrative machinery for titling remains unfunded and unstaffed.

cards.big: JAKOA's land unit has 14 officers for 200,000 Orang Asli across Peninsular Malaysia.
cards.sub: Each officer handles 14,286 individuals. Processing one title application takes 18-36 months.

cards.big: Orang Asli poverty rate is 33.6% — 6 times the national average.
cards.sub: Without land titles, communities cannot access agricultural loans, land-based subsidies, or inheritance rights.

cards.big: 0.14% coverage is not slow progress. It is administrative extinction of indigenous land rights.
Orang Asli land titling is not a resource problem — it is a priority problem. A dedicated task force with a 10-year mandate could resolve the majority of claims at modest cost.
